summary: Large code-style-only PR applying clang-format under src. The loaded evidence shows 287 changed files, 2198 additions, 1719 deletions, code-style label, empty PR body, no issue comments, no review comments, and diff excerpts consisting of whitespace, line wrapping, brace placement, pointer/cast spacing, comment spacing, and blank-line normalization rather than decomp logic changes.

key_files.role: largest listed formatting churn in changed-file metadata: 231 additions, 162 deletions, 17 hunks
large listed formatting churn: 155 additions, 88 deletions, 46 hunks
representative diff excerpt showing clang-format effects: line wrapping, brace splitting, cast spacing, comment spacing, and blank-line removal
large listed fighter formatting churn: 112 additions, 77 deletions, 17 hunks
large listed menu formatting churn: 86 additions, 85 deletions, 13 hunks
large listed header formatting churn: 66 additions, 65 deletions, 1 hunk
representative header/type formatting file listed in metadata
representative sysdolphin/baselib formatting file listed in metadata
scope of PR title and changed-file activity; full changed-file count is 287, but loaded changed_files content is truncated

what_changed: Ran clang-format on files under src, according to the PR title and changed-file scope.
Changed-file activity reports 287 files, 2198 added lines, and 1719 deleted lines.
The loaded raw PR JSON has label code-style and an empty/null body.
The loaded text corpus contains an empty PR body.
No issue comments, review comments, or reviews are present in the loaded metadata.

Representative camera.c hunks reflow long expressions and assignments across lines, split inline function signatures and opening braces, normalize pointer/cast spacing such as '(u8*) &cm_80452C68.x304' and '(s32) fn_8002F908', normalize comment spacing, and remove extra blank lines.
Representative camera.c hunk changes 'while (true) {};' into a multi-line empty loop body produced by formatting.
Changed-file metadata includes many headers and C files across camera, fighter, menu, game-mode, item, library, stage, and sysdolphin/baselib areas.
smart_moves: Kept the PR scope explicit as formatting only: title, label, empty body, and diff excerpt all point to clang-format/code-style churn rather than semantic decomp work.
Applied formatting broadly in a dedicated PR, which keeps style churn separate from functional decomp changes in review history.
Did not mix visible decomp claims, naming rationale, or validation claims into the PR body; the evidence only supports formatting cleanup.

decomp_lessons.lesson: Formatting-only churn should not be mined as evidence of authored decomp logic, symbol meaning, matching success, or reviewer preference unless comments or surrounding evidence say so.

When a diff is dominated by clang-format line wrapping, brace placement, cast spacing, and blank-line normalization, curator records should classify it as code-style cleanup and avoid inferring decomp tactics from the resulting shape.

notes: Strong evidence for PR identity, author, state, title, merge time, label, broad changed-file count, and formatting nature.
The raw diff and changed_files loaded files are truncated, so this intake cannot enumerate all 287 changed files or verify every hunk.
No review comments, reviews, issue comments, validation output, build output, objdiff output, or regression notes are present in the loaded evidence.
No naming convention or assembly/matching tactic should be inferred from this PR because the evidence indicates clang-format-only code-style churn.
The visible diff excerpt is only from camera.c, while metadata lists many additional files; file-specific claims outside camera.c are limited to changed-file counts and paths.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Treat clang-format-only PRs as style-churn evidence, not decomp-intent evidence
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2066 was a merged code-style PR titled 'Run `clang-format` on `src`' with 287 changed files and no loaded comments or review discussion. Its visible diff shows formatting changes such as line wrapping, brace placement, cast spacing, comment spacing, and blank-line normalization. Do not infer semantic decomp lessons, naming standards, matching tactics, or reviewer intent from the resulting source shape without separate evidence.

source_update_candidates.title: Formatting-only PRs should not be treated as decomp rationale
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding a curation/decomp-history note: broad clang-format or code-style-only PRs may demonstrate repository formatting preferences, but they should not be used as evidence for semantic naming, control-flow, typing, matching tactics, validation success, or reviewer intent unless the PR discussion or adjacent evidence explicitly supports that interpretation.
evidence_refs: camera.c diff excerpt showing formatting-only transformations
source_update_candidates.reason: This PR provides a clear example of large formatting churn that should be separated from decomp knowledge extraction; curator should decide whether this belongs in standards or intake guidance.

rejection_notes: Do not add naming conventions from this PR; no rename rationale or semantic naming evidence is present.
Do not add matching tactics from this PR; no objdiff, checkdiff, build, assembly, permuter, or match-status evidence is present.
Do not infer reviewer approval preferences beyond the merge metadata; there are no loaded review comments or reviews.
Do not use camera.c's post-format line breaks as evidence that those expression shapes were authored for matching; the PR title and label indicate automated formatting.
